identificationModule.briefTitle: The Effect of Eplerenone on the Evolution of Vasculopathy in Renal Transplant Patients.
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized Crossover Clinical Trial Regarding the Blockage of the Mineralocorticoid Receptor Using Eplerenone on the Evolution of Arterial Stiffness in Kidney Patients One Year After Transplant

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
Cardiovascular (CV) pathologies are the leading cause of death in kidney transplant patients.Arterial stiffness is a prognostic factor for CV mortality in kidney transplantation. Despite a reduced CV risk in transplant kidney patients in comparison to patients in dialysis, CV mortality among kidney transplant patients is much higher than the general population.

After renal transplantation, the cardiac and vascular anomalies observed in chronic end-stage renal disease are partially improved because of restored normal kidney function and withdrawal from dialysis.

However, patients are exposed to immunosuppressive drugs, in particular calcineurin inhibitors, which can be associated with vascular toxicity, either directly or by promoting the appearance of hypertension, diabetes, or dyslipidemia.The pathophysiology of arterial stiffness in kidney transplantation is complex and multifactorial.

Calcineurin inhibitors are likely to play an important role in the persistence of increased arterial stiffness in transplant patients in whom renal function has been restored. Indeed, the discontinuation of anti-calcineurins in favour of other molecules .is associated with a decrease of arterial stiffness.

Preclinical work has shown that the vascular toxicity of cyclosporine is mediated by activation of the mineralocorticoid receptor in smooth muscle cells. The involvement of the mineralocorticoid receptor in the onset of arterial stiffness is also well demonstrated in non-transplanted subjects.

Blocking the mineralocorticoid receptor in patients under cyclosporine may reduce their arterial stiffness and in and consequently improve their CV prognosis.

Studies have show a good safety in kidney transplant patients. This pilot study proposes to examine, for the first time, the impact of treatment with a mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist on the evolution of arterial stiffness in renal transplant patients on calcineurin inhibitors.

conditions: Kidney Transplantation for More Than One Year
Patients with a Kidney Transplantation on Cyclosporine

designInfo.interventionModelDescription:
* Arm A in which they will receive eplerenone 50mg/day taken orally for 6 months, followed by a 8 to 10 weeks wash-out period, then a 6-month period without eplerenone, until the end of the study.
* arm B where they will be eplerenone-free for 6 months, followed by a 8 to 10 weeks wash-out period, then a 6-month period in which they will receive eplerenone 50 mg/day as a single dose taken orally.

armGroups.label: Eplerenone group A (cross over design)
armGroups.description: Patient will receive eplerenone 50mg/day taken orally for 6 months, followed by a 8 to 10 weeks wash-out period, then a 6-month period without eplerenone, until the end of the study.
interventionNames: Other: Period without eplerenone (cross over design)

armGroups.label: Eplerenone group B (cross over design)
armGroups.description: Eplerenone-free for 6 months, followed by a 8 to 10 weeks wash-out period, then a 6-month period in which patients will receive eplerenone 50 mg/day as a single dose taken orally.
interventionNames: Other: Period without eplerenone (cross over design)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Evolution of pulse wave velocity (PWV in m/s) adjusted to the blood pressure

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Evolution of graft function
secondaryOutcomes.description: measured by creatinine (in micromol/L) with estimation of glomerular filtration rate (eGFR in mL/min/1.73m2 ) according to the CKD-EPI formula.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Evolution of proteinuria
secondaryOutcomes.description: measured by the ratio proteinuria/creatinuria (in mg/g)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of patients with DFG ≥ 90, 60-89, 45-59, 30-44, 15-29 <15ml/min/1,73m2
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of patient with ratio proteinuria/creatinuria (en mg/g) <500 ; 500-1000, 1000-2000, 2000-3000, >3000
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 6 months of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: hyperkalemia occurence ≥ 5.5 mmol/L
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: during 6 month of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Number of hyperkalemia
secondaryOutcomes.description: number of hyperkalemias during hyperkalemia follow-up between 5 - 5.49; 5.5 - 6; \>6mmol/L
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: during 6 month of treatment with eplerenone

secondaryOutcomes.measure: increase of creatinine of more than 50%
secondaryOutcomes.description: Evaluation of risk of acute renal failure defined as an increase in creatinine of more than 50%
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: during 6 month of treatment with eplerenone
